steps.title: Set up the inequality from Ohm's law
steps.content:
Use Ohm's law, $I=\frac{V}{R}$. The problem gives:
  • Resistance: $R=500$ ohms
  • Potential difference: $V=6n$ volts
  • Current limit: $I\leq 0.25$ amperes
Substitute those values into $I=\frac{V}{R}$:$$\frac{6n}{500}\leq 0.25$$

steps.title: Solve for n by isolating the variable
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by 500:
$$6n \leq 0.25 \times 500$$
$$6n \leq 125$$
Divide both sides by 6:
$$n \leq \frac{125}{6} = 20.8\overline{3}$$
Since n must be a whole number of batteries, the greatest number of six-volt batteries that can be used is 20.
